article_meta.title: Metro An open letter from the heart of Iran to America – Don’t give up on us By California Post Staff Published March 7, 2026, 7:11 p.m

reasoning: The model flagged bias from an extended first-person open letter (source voice) rather than the journalist’s own prose, violating the attribution rule. It also missed the primary author-level framing (e.g., emotive headline/structural one-sidedness).

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Both flagged passages come from the source’s open-letter narrative, not the author’s connecting prose, which the rule explicitly forbids.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: All flagged items are not genuine author-level bias; the first also misuses 'Bias by Omission' at the sentence level.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed plausible author-level framing such as the emotive, advocacy-style headline and the article’s one-sided story choice (publishing only the open letter without countervailing context).
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: One label (Opinion as Fact) could be defensible if it were the author’s voice; the other (Bias by Omission) is misapplied. Overall label accuracy is weak given misattribution.
